design_philosophy.core_loop: Beat-em-up combat cycle: advance through stages, defeat waves of enemies, confront stage bosses, progress to next stage
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing character positioning and crowd control while advancing through enemy-filled environments
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody a futuristic commando operative clearing hostile territory with special combat abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Port of arcade game with character-driven design philosophy; each of four playable characters has distinct combat identity and moveset

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with game over state
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; loss of lives leads to stage restart
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection affects difficulty via different movesets and combat ranges; four distinct playable characters provide varied mechanical challenges

progression.upgrade_system: Special moves and abilities tied to character selection; limited in-stage power-ups and temporary buffs
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character-gated progression; all four characters available from start but with distinct playstyles requiring player adaptation
replayability_hooks: Four distinct playable characters with different combat mechanics
Character mastery and optimization of special moves
Arcade-style high-score chase and speedrun potential

world_building_techniques: Character naming and theming (futuristic commando squad concept)
Stage progression implies geographical campaign through hostile territory
Boss encounter structure suggests escalating threat hierarchy

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-culminating encounters with distinct visual identity; boss patterns designed around arcade-era memorization and positioning
notable_bosses: Scumocide (final boss; regional censorship renaming from 'Genocide')
Various stage-specific bosses with thematic design reflecting stage environments

faction_indicators: Four-character squad composition suggests team-based commando unit
Character archetypes: Mack, Ginzu, Baby, Hoover represent specialized commando roles
danger_signals: Regional censorship of enemy names ('Nooky' to 'Wooky', 'Genocide' to 'Scumocide') indicates localization-era content sensitivity
Boss naming convention ('Boots', 'Scumocide') suggests cartoonic rather than realistic violence framing
environmental_storytelling: Stage progression implies military/tactical campaign structure
Enemy types and boss encounters suggest escalating organizational hierarchy to dismantle

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Captain Commando (SNES)
